title: Exacum filiforme
taxonomy: Exacum filiforme is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is a type of gentian that is native to parts of Asia. This species is known for its delicate, thread-like leaves and small white flowers. Exacum filiforme is classified as a perennial herb, which means it grows and blooms year after year. The exact classification of Exacum filiforme can also be broken down into its binomial nomenclature, specifically Exacum filiforme, which is used in botanical classification.

Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, Exacum filiforme is believed to have antibacterial properties, making it effective against certain infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of this plant.
